subtopics.subtopic_description: Educational background or training
notes: Currently pursuing a Bachelor's degree in Mathematics and Computer Science at Chulalongkorn University
Holds an Associate Degree, completed prior to current university studies
Has completed coursework in data science, programming (Python), and statistics
Participated in university-led workshops on machine learning and data analysis

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific job title and role description
notes: Works as a Data Entry Keyer on student projects and freelance assignments
Also acts as a junior data scientist on academic and student-led research projects
Responsible for converting paper-based documents to digital formats and maintaining data integrity
Assists in database creation and maintenance for hotel management and other projects

subtopics.subtopic_description: Current industry or sector (e.g., tech, finance, manufacturing)
notes: Primarily operates within the technology and academic research sector
Focuses on data science applications, including machine learning and data entry
Has experience with hospitality sector data (hotel management project)
Engages in both educational and practical data projects

subtopics.subtopic_description: Company size and environment
notes: Works mostly in small project teams (3-6 people), typical for university and freelance work
Academic environment with close collaboration among peers and supervisors
Project-based work with variable team composition
No formal corporate structure; projects are managed by students and faculty

subtopics.subtopic_description: Type of business or market segment
notes: Student projects focus on technology-driven data solutions
Hotel management project relates to the hospitality market segment
Most work centers around digital transformation of manual processes
Occasional freelance data entry for local businesses

subtopics.subtopic_description: Duration/years of experience in current role
notes: Has less than one year of professional experience in data entry and data science roles
Began working on data-driven projects during the first year at university
Recent exposure to both academic and practical data entry scenarios
Seeks additional experience through coursework and side projects

subtopics.subtopic_description: Professional seniority or career level
notes: Entry-level/junior professional, primarily a student worker
Acts as a team member rather than project lead
Regularly consults with more experienced peers and faculty for guidance
Aims to build foundational skills for advancement to more senior roles

subtopics.subtopic_description: Primary job responsibilities and regular daily tasks
notes: Performs data cleaning, preprocessing, and validation for machine learning datasets
Enters and organizes data from paper documents into digital databases
Develops Python scripts for automating repetitive data tasks
Creates user interfaces for simplified data entry in custom applications

subtopics.subtopic_description: Approximate proportion of time spent on core activities
notes: Spends approximately 50% of time on data cleaning and preprocessing
About 30% of time is dedicated to manual data entry and categorization
Invests 10% of time in developing and testing simple machine learning models
Remaining 10% is spent on user interface design and documentation

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of autonomy and scope of decision-making in the role
notes: Works under guidance of project supervisors or team leads
Has autonomy in choosing data cleaning methods and tool selection within project guidelines
Makes independent decisions on data categorization and duplicate removal
Collaborates with team members for major database design or workflow changes

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that feel easiest or most natural to perform
notes: Data entry from structured documents is straightforward and can be done efficiently
Writing basic Python scripts for simple data manipulation comes naturally
Using Excel or CSV tools for preliminary data review feels intuitive
Organizing digital files and folders for project data is easy to manage

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks perceived as most challenging or complex
notes: Handling class imbalance and missing values in machine learning datasets is challenging
Designing user interfaces for non-technical users requires additional effort
Integrating new data sources into existing databases can be complex
Ensuring data accuracy when converting from paper to digital formats can be difficult

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that are repetitive, data-heavy, or suitable for automation
notes: Manual entry of large volumes of hotel records from paper forms is repetitive
Basic data cleaning (removing duplicates, formatting columns) is data-heavy and suited for Python automation
Converting scanned documents to digital text using OCR is repetitive but can be automated
Routine database updates with new records can be streamlined with scripts

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that are most enjoyable or engaging versus those that feel boring or tedious
notes: Enjoys building machine learning models and analyzing their performance metrics
Finds designing new workflows and automation scripts engaging
Feels bored by repetitive manual data entry from paper documents
Testing and debugging user interfaces is more engaging than raw data entry

subtopics.subtopic_description: Common pain points or inefficiencies in completing tasks
notes: Dealing with inconsistent paper record formats slows down data entry
Manual data cleaning is time-consuming and error-prone
Limited access to high-quality scanned documents can hinder automation
Occasional software integration issues between data entry tools and databases

subtopics.subtopic_description: How enjoyment, skill level, and productivity relate to one another
notes: Productivity is highest when working on Python scripting and automation tasks
Enjoyment increases when tasks leverage skills in data science and programming
Tedious manual entry reduces both enjoyment and perceived productivity
Learning new data analysis techniques enhances both skill and engagement

subtopics.subtopic_description: Attitude towards learning new technologies and tools
notes: Has a positive attitude towards adopting new software and programming tools
Enjoys experimenting with new Python libraries for data analysis
Sees learning new tech as essential for future career growth
Actively seeks out tutorials and online resources to expand technical knowledge

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived adaptability to new software/methods
notes: Considers adaptability to new tools a personal strength
Able to pick up basic functionality of new platforms within a few hours
Adapts quickly to changes in project requirements or workflows
Occasionally requires peer support for advanced features

subtopics.subtopic_description: Willingness to invest time in tech training
notes: Willing to dedicate several hours per week to learning new programming techniques
Participates in online courses and university workshops related to data science
Believes that time spent on tech training pays off in increased efficiency
Tries to balance training time with academic workload

subtopics.subtopic_description: Motivations or barriers to learning new tech (e.g., workload, relevance)
notes: Motivated by desire to automate repetitive tasks and improve workflows
Seeks to stay current with industry tools relevant to data science
Occasional high academic workload can limit time for tech exploration
Sometimes hesitant to learn tools perceived as less relevant to immediate tasks

subtopics.subtopic_description: Influence of peers or management on willingness to adopt new tools
notes: Peers in project teams often recommend new libraries or techniques
Faculty supervisors encourage experimentation with new software
Collaboration with tech-savvy classmates increases openness to adoption
Project requirements sometimes necessitate learning new platforms

topic: Primary Tools and Technologies Used in Work

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific software, platforms, or systems used daily
notes: Uses Python (pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn) for data cleaning and analysis
Regularly works with Microsoft Excel for initial data review and transformation
Utilizes SQL databases to store and query structured data
Occasionally employs OCR tools to convert scanned documents to text

subtopics.subtopic_description: Essential non-AI tools for workflow
notes: Relies on Excel for quick data validation and manual adjustments
Uses Git for version control of scripts and project files
Employs Google Drive for collaborative document sharing
Applies basic text editors (e.g., VS Code) for code development

subtopics.subtopic_description: Familiarity with industry-standard technologies
notes: Familiar with Jupyter Notebook for interactive data exploration
Understands SQL syntax and basic database operations
Has basic knowledge of cloud-based data storage solutions
Learning to use version control systems as standard practice

subtopics.subtopic_description: Interoperability or integration issues between tools
notes: Sometimes encounters formatting issues when moving data between Excel and SQL
Occasional incompatibility between OCR outputs and database import scripts
Python scripts sometimes require additional libraries for full tool integration
Manual intervention needed when automated tools misinterpret document layouts

subtopics.subtopic_description: Familiarity with fundamental AI/ML concepts and terminology
notes: Comfortable with terms like classification, regression, and deep learning
Understands the basics of supervised and unsupervised learning
Familiar with concepts of data imbalance, overfitting, and validation
Can explain difference between traditional programming and machine learning approaches

subtopics.subtopic_description: Names of specific AI software/platforms currently used in work
notes: Uses OpenAI's ChatGPT for coding assistance and idea generation
Employs scikit-learn for machine learning model building
Utilizes pandas for data preparation and manipulation
Experiments with TensorFlow and Keras in deep learning coursework

subtopics.subtopic_description: Frequency and purpose of AI tool application (specific use cases)
notes: Uses LLMs like ChatGPT weekly for debugging and code review
Applies machine learning libraries to build and evaluate classification models
Leverages AI tools to automate text extraction from scanned documents
Consults AI assistants for workflow optimization suggestions

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific examples of AI success and failure experiences (lessons learned)
notes: Successfully used ChatGPT to quickly generate boilerplate code for data cleaning scripts
Faced challenges when AI misinterpreted poorly scanned documents during OCR
Improved machine learning model accuracy after using AI-generated feature engineering ideas
Learned that AI tools require careful human oversight to avoid subtle data entry errors

subtopics.subtopic_description: Availability of organizational training or peer resources for AI use
notes: No formal corporate training, but university offers AI/ML workshops
Peers frequently share online resources and code snippets
Faculty advisors provide guidance on best practices for AI tool usage
Self-directed learning through online tutorials is common

topic: AI Interaction Style and Workflow Change

subtopics.subtopic_description: Preferred mode of interaction: independent versus step-by-step collaboration
notes: Prefers step-by-step collaboration with AI tools for complex tasks
Often starts independently, then consults AI for specific problems or suggestions
Enjoys experimenting with AI-generated code before full integration
Values iterative feedback between self and AI assistants

subtopics.subtopic_description: Style of human-AI teaming (e.g., advisor, assistant, co-worker)
notes: Views AI primarily as an assistant for routine and repetitive tasks
Treats AI as an advisor when exploring new technical concepts or debugging
Sees AI as a co-worker when brainstorming project workflow improvements
Maintains final decision-making authority on all project outputs

subtopics.subtopic_description: Willingness and openness to adopting new AI-driven workflows
notes: Highly open to integrating AI-driven processes that increase efficiency
Willing to experiment with new AI features as they become available
Actively seeks ways to automate manual data entry using AI tools
Evaluates risks and benefits before fully adopting new AI workflows

subtopics.subtopic_description: Preference for conversational vs. command-based interfaces (communication dynamics)
notes: Prefers conversational interfaces (e.g., ChatGPT) for brainstorming and problem-solving
Uses command-based interfaces (e.g., Python scripts) for precise technical tasks
Finds natural language prompts helpful for exploring unfamiliar topics
Believes conversational AI lowers barriers to entry for new users

subtopics.subtopic_description: Extent to which tasks rely on specialized, tacit domain knowledge
notes: Most data entry tasks are procedural and require limited tacit knowledge
Some machine learning model tasks require understanding of statistical principles
Domain knowledge is important when categorizing ambiguous hotel records
Relies on supervisors for input on domain-specific data interpretation

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of trust in AI outputs for work tasks and critical decisions
notes: Trusts AI for routine data cleaning and formatting, with spot checks for accuracy
Skeptical of AI outputs when source data is unstructured or low-quality
Validates AI-generated results before including them in final project deliverables
Believes that critical decisions should involve human review

subtopics.subtopic_description: Ideal balance of human effort and AI automation for specific tasks
notes: Prefers high automation for repetitive data entry and cleaning
Favors human oversight for data categorization and model evaluation
Sees value in AI-human collaboration during exploratory data analysis
Believes manual intervention should be available for edge cases

subtopics.subtopic_description: Conditions under which high automation is acceptable or threatening
notes: Automation is acceptable when data is well-structured and rules are clear
Feels threatened by automation if it reduces learning opportunities or job relevance
Accepts high automation for tasks prone to human error or tedium
Resists automation when it risks misinterpretation of nuanced data

topic: AI Impact on Skills and Job Security

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived impact of AI on the importance of existing skills (enhanced vs. reduced)
notes: Believes AI enhances the importance of programming and data analysis skills
Routine data entry skills are becoming less critical due to automation
Analytical thinking and problem-solving remain highly valued
Sees a shift towards skills in AI tool management and oversight

subtopics.subtopic_description: Emerging skills or new areas of responsibility created by AI
notes: Developing skills in prompt engineering and conversational AI interaction
Takes responsibility for validating and interpreting AI-generated outputs
Learning to integrate multiple AI tools into cohesive workflows
Exploring ethical considerations in automated data processing

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of concern about AI replacing specific tasks or the overall role
notes: Moderately concerned about automation replacing manual data entry
Believes analytical and creative tasks remain less vulnerable to AI takeover
Willing to adapt by upskilling in more technical and supervisory areas
Sees AI as a tool for career growth rather than a threat to overall employment

subtopics.subtopic_description: Availability of override mechanisms or manual checks for AI-driven processes
notes: Implements manual reviews of AI-processed data before database entry
Develops verification scripts to cross-check AI-generated outputs
Peers or supervisors are involved in final validation for critical tasks
Human intervention is always possible before final submission

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived change in team or company policies regarding AI adoption
notes: Team is increasingly open to experimenting with new AI tools
No strict policies limit AI use in student projects, as long as outputs are validated
Faculty encourage responsible AI adoption and ethical considerations
Peer discussions about AI best practices are becoming more common

subtopics.subtopic_description: General outlook on AI's broader societal and industry impact
notes: Sees AI as transformative for data-driven industries and society
Believes AI will improve efficiency and accuracy in many fields
Recognizes potential for job displacement but also new opportunity creation
Thinks ongoing education is key to adapting to AI-driven changes

subtopics.subtopic_description: Personal beliefs about the ethics and risks of AI in the workplace
notes: Believes ethical use of AI requires transparency and human oversight
Concerned about potential biases in AI-driven decision-making
Supports clear guidelines for responsible data use in projects
Thinks AI should assist, not fully replace, human workers

subtopics.subtopic_description: Missing AI tools or features that would be most beneficial in the future
notes: Would benefit from AI tools with better handling of low-quality scanned documents
Desires AI that can automatically identify and resolve data inconsistencies
Wants smarter database integration features to streamline data import/export
Envisions AI that can provide personalized learning recommendations for upskilling

subtopics.subtopic_description: Predicted evolution of their job in the next 5-10 years with AI integration
notes: Expects shift from manual data entry to data quality assurance and AI tool management
Foresees increased need for expertise in AI workflow integration
Predicts a focus on data analysis and interpretation, rather than basic entry
Believes project management and ethical oversight roles will grow in importance

subtopics.subtopic_description: Concrete steps they would want their organization to take regarding AI strategy
notes: Advocates for regular training sessions on new AI tools and best practices
Suggests establishing guidelines for ethical AI use and human oversight
Wants improved access to shared AI resources and collaborative platforms
Recommends incentives for students and team members who innovate with AI
